description: Compute a composite Redevelopment Potential Index (RPI) for San Francisco parcels using UrbanSim data in the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/redevelopment_potential.ipynb. Using the 'buildings', 'parcels', and 'zoning' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, calculate the RPI as: 0.4 * underutilization_score + 0.35 * age_score + 0.25 * value_score. The underutilization score is 1 - (actual_far / max_far) clipped to [0, 1], where actual FAR is the sum of building_sqft divided by parcel shape_area. The age score is the min-max normalized average year_built per parcel, where older buildings receive higher scores. The value intensity score is the inverted min-max normalized average residential_sales_price per unit area. Save the top 50 parcels by RPI to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_redevelopment_parcels.csv, including columns for parcel_id, underutilization_score, age_score, value_score, rpi, and rank. Generate a scatter plot of underutilization_score (x) vs age_score (y) colored by rpi and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/rpi_scatter.png. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
